game_name: Dragon Quest V: Hand of the Heavenly Bride

design_philosophy.core_loop: Turn-based party combat with monster recruitment, exploration, leveling, equipment progression, and life-stage narrative spanning ~30 years
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Party composition strategy (human vs. recruited monsters) balanced against growing difficulty; narrative tension of uncovering father's past and rescuing mother
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Build a custom monster army while experiencing a multi-generational life story; progress from child to adult hero with family
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Pioneering monster recruitment system (40-71 recruitable monsters) as party alternative to fixed human roster; first Dragon Quest game featuring playable pregnancy and multi-decade life narrative; wagon-based party management emphasizing strategic depth in formation

difficulty.checkpointing: Wagon resting system allows recovery between dungeons; saving at temples/inns
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency via tactics system—players can set AI routines individually per ally or control manually for entire game; monster recruitment choices directly impact party capability and available strategies

progression.upgrade_system: Experience-based leveling for hero and party members (human and recruited monsters); equipment acquisition through shops and enemy drops; monster leveling mirrors human character progression
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated progression through life stages (child, enslaved youth, adult adventurer, married family man); monster recruitment unlocks as party strength increases; bonus dungeon post-game
replayability_hooks: Monster recruitment system allows different party compositions
Bride choice affects narrative (multiple marriage options)
Tactics customization encourages different approach strategies

world_building_techniques: Character-driven plot revealed through dialogue (father's past, mother's rescue)
Environmental progression showing world from child and adult perspectives
Family-building mechanic (marriage, children) integrated into gameplay
World exploration tied to narrative discovery

faction_indicators: Monster-recruiting vs. human-party philosophy (character philosophy signal)
danger_signals: Slavery arc and forced labor (moral backstory element)
environmental_storytelling: Wagon as mobile base showing party composition and resources
Childhood hometown vs. adult world locations
Temple construction project (sign of enslavement and societal structure)
Monster encounters offering recruitment (world feels populated with agency)
